goal: Open Phase 1 Week 4 by landing the ucil-core SQLite knowledge-graph schema (WAL + busy_timeout=10000 + BEGIN IMMEDIATE writes; tables for symbols, knowledge_entries, hot_staging, warm, quality_issues, feedback, conventions, sessions per master-plan §12.1), and add the missing module-level test in ucil-daemon/src/server.rs that asserts the four CEQP universal parameters are present on every one of the 22 tool schemas.
plan_summary: Part 1 (ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs): NEW module with `KnowledgeGraph { conn: rusqlite::Connection }`, `KnowledgeGraphError` (thiserror), `pub fn open(db_path: &Path) -> Result<Self, KnowledgeGraphError>` that opens the connection, applies `PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL`, `PRAGMA busy_timeout=10000`, `PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON`, then runs the §12.1 init DDL inside a single `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` transaction. The DDL creates the eight logical groups required by the feature description: (1) symbols → `entities` + `relations` (master-plan §12.1 lines 1130-1159), (2) knowledge_entries → `decisions` + `observations` (lines 1161-1194), (3) conventions → `conventions` (lines 1172-1182), (4) quality_issues → `quality_issues` (lines 1197-1211), (5) hot_staging → `hot_observations` + `hot_convention_signals` + `hot_architecture_deltas` + `hot_decision_material` (lines 1213-1251), (6) warm → `warm_observations` + `warm_conventions` + `warm_architecture_state` + `warm_decisions` (lines 1253-1293), (7) feedback → `feedback_signals` (lines 1295-1303), (8) sessions → a new `sessions` table inferred from §11.2 line 1081 + the SessionInfo extension landed on the WO-0008 branch (id PK, agent_id, branch, worktree_root, created_at, last_active, inferred_domain, expires_at). Indexes from lines 1306-1318 are created in the same transaction. `KnowledgeGraph::open` is idempotent — the init DDL uses `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` and `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS`. Module-level `#[cfg(test)] #[test] fn test_schema_creation()` (NOT nested under `mod tests`) exercises the full path: open against a tempdir SQLite file, assert journal_mode==wal, assert busy_timeout==10000, query `sqlite_master` for each expected table name, assert all eight logical groups have at least their lead table, assert a `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` write succeeds. Part 2 (ucil-daemon/src/server.rs): ADD a single module-level `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test] async fn test_ceqp_params_on_all_tools()` (peer to the existing module-level `test_all_22_tools_registered`, NOT inside `mod tests`) that calls `ucil_tools()`, iterates the returned `Vec<ToolDescriptor>`, and asserts every tool's `input_schema.properties` contains the four CEQP keys `reason`, `current_task`, `files_in_context`, `token_budget` per master-plan §8.2. No new logic is added to the schema — WO-0010 already wired `ceqp_input_schema()` into every descriptor; this WO only adds the frozen-selector regression test that P1-W4-F06's acceptance selector requires.
scope_in: crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — NEW module. Public API: `pub struct KnowledgeGraph { conn: rusqlite::Connection }`; `pub enum KnowledgeGraphError` (thiserror, `#[non_exhaustive]`, variants `Sqlite(#[from] rusqlite::Error)` and `Io { path: PathBuf, source: std::io::Error }`); `impl KnowledgeGraph { pub fn open(db_path: &Path) -> Result<Self, KnowledgeGraphError>; pub fn conn(&self) -> &rusqlite::Connection; pub fn execute_in_transaction<F, T>(&mut self, f: F) -> Result<T, KnowledgeGraphError> where F: FnOnce(&rusqlite::Transaction) -> Result<T, rusqlite::Error> }`. The transaction helper MUST start the transaction with `Connection::transaction_with_behavior(TransactionBehavior::Immediate)` (rusqlite's API equivalent of `BEGIN IMMEDIATE`) per master-plan §11 line 1117 (the #1 cause of unexpected SQLite concurrency failures is the default `BEGIN DEFERRED`).

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::open` MUST: (1) open the connection with `Connection::open(db_path)?`; (2) execute `PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;` and verify the returned mode equals `wal` (case-insensitive); (3) execute `PRAGMA busy_timeout = 10000;`; (4) execute `PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;`; (5) run the full §12.1 init DDL inside a single `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` transaction using the helper above. All pragmas use `query_row` / `pragma_query` per rusqlite idioms — never `unwrap()` outside test code (.claude/rules/rust-style.md).

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — embed the init DDL as a `const INIT_SQL: &str` (or a private `fn init_sql() -> &'static str`). The DDL MUST create, in this exact order: `entities`, `relations`, `decisions`, `conventions`, `observations`, `quality_issues`, `hot_observations`, `hot_convention_signals`, `hot_architecture_deltas`, `hot_decision_material`, `warm_observations`, `warm_conventions`, `warm_architecture_state`, `warm_decisions`, `feedback_signals`, and one new `sessions` table (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, agent_id TEXT, branch TEXT, worktree_root TEXT, created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')), last_active TEXT, inferred_domain TEXT, expires_at INTEGER). All `CREATE TABLE` and `CREATE INDEX` statements MUST use `IF NOT EXISTS` so `KnowledgeGraph::open` is idempotent. Reproduce the §12.1 column types verbatim from master-plan lines 1130-1303 — do not paraphrase. Reproduce the indexes from lines 1306-1318 verbatim plus add `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sessions_branch ON sessions(branch);` and `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sessions_expires ON sessions(expires_at);`.

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — module-level acceptance test `test_schema_creation`. The frozen selector for P1-W4-F01 is the EXACT path `knowledge_graph::test_schema_creation`. It MUST be a stand-alone `#[cfg(test)] #[test] fn test_schema_creation()` at module level — NOT inside a `mod tests { … }` block (nesting it produces `knowledge_graph::tests::test_schema_creation`, which the selector will NOT match — see escalation 20260415-1856 + WO-0007 precedent). Test body: (1) open a `tempfile::TempDir`, create a new `KnowledgeGraph` against `<tempdir>/knowledge.db`; (2) query `PRAGMA journal_mode` and assert the result equals `wal` (case-insensitive); (3) query `PRAGMA busy_timeout` and assert it equals 10000; (4) query `sqlite_master` for each of the 16 table names listed above and assert each is present; (5) call `execute_in_transaction` with a trivial INSERT into `sessions` and assert the row count == 1; (6) re-open the same db file and assert the schema persists (idempotency check). Use `tempfile::TempDir`, never `/tmp` literals.

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — additional in-file `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { … }` block is OPTIONAL and reserved for SUPPORTING tests with selectors of the form `knowledge_graph::tests::*` (which are non-frozen). If you add it, do NOT put `test_schema_creation` inside it — that test alone must remain at module level.

crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs — add `pub mod knowledge_graph;` and `pub use knowledge_graph::{KnowledgeGraph, KnowledgeGraphError};` re-exports. Keep lib.rs logic-free per .claude/rules/rust-style.md (re-exports + module declarations only).

crates/ucil-core/Cargo.toml — `rusqlite` is already a workspace dep; no new top-level dep is required. If you find that `rusqlite::TransactionBehavior::Immediate` is not in scope, add the explicit `use rusqlite::TransactionBehavior;` import inside knowledge_graph.rs. Do NOT add `rusqlite` features beyond what the workspace declares (`bundled` is already set in workspace Cargo.toml).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs — ADD a single module-level acceptance test `test_ceqp_params_on_all_tools` (frozen selector `server::test_ceqp_params_on_all_tools`, exact match — MUST live at module level, not under `mod tests`). The test MUST: (1) call the existing `pub fn ucil_tools() -> Vec<ToolDescriptor>` to get all 22 descriptors; (2) assert `tools.len() == 22` (sanity); (3) for each tool, parse `tool.input_schema` as a `serde_json::Value::Object` and assert that the `properties` sub-object contains the four CEQP keys: `reason`, `current_task`, `files_in_context`, `token_budget`; (4) for each CEQP property, assert its `type` field matches the expected JSON-Schema type (`string`, `string`, `array`, `integer`) per master-plan §8.2; (5) collect any failing tool name(s) into a Vec and assert it is empty so the failure message lists every offender at once. Place the new test as a peer to the existing module-level `test_all_22_tools_registered` (NOT inside any `mod tests` block, to satisfy the exact-match selector — same pattern as DEC-0005 / WO-0007 / WO-0010).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs — do NOT modify `ucil_tools()`, `ceqp_input_schema()`, `ToolDescriptor`, or any other existing pub item. The test must verify the EXISTING wiring; if it fails, the fix lives in `ceqp_input_schema()` (which already lists the four CEQP properties from WO-0010 and is verified by `test_all_22_tools_registered`).
crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs — no edits required; `server` module is already exported by WO-0010.

Per-crate rustdoc on every new pub item (KnowledgeGraph, KnowledgeGraphError, open, conn, execute_in_transaction). Each `Result`-returning pub fn includes a `# Errors` section per .claude/rules/rust-style.md.
Commits follow .claude/rules/commit-style.md with `Phase: 1`, `Feature: P1-W4-F01` or `Feature: P1-W4-F06`, `Work-order: WO-0011` trailers.
scope_out: P1-W4-F02 (knowledge_graph CRUD + bi-temporal queries + symbol resolution) — depends on this WO; goes in a follow-up WO.

P1-W4-F03, P1-W4-F04, P1-W4-F05, P1-W4-F08, P1-W4-F09, P1-W4-F10 — separate WOs; do NOT add CRUD methods, knowledge-graph population, or session-deduplication logic in this WO. The test_schema_creation acceptance only proves the schema and pragmas are correct.
Wiring `KnowledgeGraph::open` from `crates/ucil-daemon/src/main.rs` — main.rs is reserved for the integration WO (P1-W3-F08 progressive startup); do NOT touch it here.
Editing `crates/ucil-daemon/src/storage.rs` — that file lives on the WO-0006 branch awaiting re-verification. Touching it here would create a merge conflict.

Editing `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lifecycle.rs` or `crates/ucil-daemon/src/session_manager.rs` — those live on the WO-0008 branch awaiting re-verification (DEC-0007 removed the mutation-gate that blocked it). Do NOT touch.
Editing `crates/ucil-treesitter/src/symbols.rs` or `chunker.rs` — those live on the WO-0006 branch awaiting re-verification. Do NOT touch.
Wiring `KnowledgeGraph` to tree-sitter extraction — that's P1-W4-F04, separate WO.
Cross-database joins, attached databases — out of scope for the schema-creation feature.

P1-W4-F06: `cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon server::test_ceqp_params_on_all_tools` reports exactly one passing test.
Schema persists across connections: re-opening the same db file does not re-create or duplicate any table (idempotent init via `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`).
Pragmas applied: `journal_mode=wal`, `busy_timeout=10000`, `foreign_keys=on` on every opened connection.
Writes use `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` (per master-plan §11 line 1117) — `execute_in_transaction` opens with `TransactionBehavior::Immediate`.
No mocks of rusqlite or sqlite3 — tests run against real on-disk SQLite files in tempdirs.

cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon server::test_ceqp_params_on_all_tools --no-fail-fast — exits 0, exactly 1 test resolved and passed.

grep -E 'PRAGMA busy_timeout\s*=\s*10000' crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs returns at least one match.

grep -cE 'CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS (entities|relations|decisions|conventions|observations|quality_issues|hot_observations|hot_convention_signals|hot_architecture_deltas|hot_decision_material|warm_observations|warm_conventions|warm_architecture_state|warm_decisions|feedback_signals|sessions)' crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs is ≥ 16 (all 16 tables present and idempotent).
scripts/reality-check.sh P1-W4-F01 exits 0 (or, if the per-file rollback false-positive trips per escalation 20260415-1630, manual verification by the verifier is the documented workaround).
scripts/reality-check.sh P1-W4-F06 exits 0 (same caveat as above).
implementation_notes: RUSQLITE PRAGMAS: `conn.pragma_update(None, "journal_mode", "WAL")?` returns the new mode as a `String` you can assert against (case-insensitive). For `busy_timeout`, use `conn.pragma_update(None, "busy_timeout", 10_000_i64)?` then `conn.pragma_query(None, "busy_timeout", |row| row.get::<_, i64>(0))?`. For `foreign_keys`, `conn.pragma_update(None, "foreign_keys", "ON")?`.

BEGIN IMMEDIATE: rusqlite exposes `Connection::transaction_with_behavior(TransactionBehavior::Immediate)`. Inside the closure, `tx.execute_batch(INIT_SQL)?` runs the entire DDL atomically; `tx.commit()?` finalises. Every WRITE elsewhere in the codebase will use the same helper.

FROZEN SELECTOR LESSON (WO-0006 / WO-0007 / WO-0008 / WO-0010): both acceptance tests in this WO use the EXACT-match selector form (`module::fn_name`, no trailing `::`). Nextest treats `::tests::` as a distinct path segment. Placing either test inside the existing `mod tests { … }` block will cause the verifier to reject. The fix is structural: keep the test as a stand-alone `#[cfg(test)] #[test]` (or `#[tokio::test]`) at module level, anywhere outside `mod tests { … }`. This was the root cause of WO-0006 retry-3 reject and is called out explicitly in DEC-0005 + the WO-0010 plan.

MOST OF THE DDL IS ALREADY IN MASTER-PLAN §12.1 — copy lines 1130-1318 verbatim into INIT_SQL and add the `IF NOT EXISTS` clause to every CREATE statement plus the new `sessions` table. Do not paraphrase column names or types; the verifier will diff your DDL against the master plan during the critic pass.

SESSIONS TABLE INFERENCE: master-plan §12.1 does not enumerate a `sessions` table explicitly — the feature description bundles it into the eight logical groups, and §11.2 line 1081 references `.ucil/sessions/`. The minimum viable schema for this WO is: `id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, agent_id TEXT, branch TEXT, worktree_root TEXT, created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')), last_active TEXT, inferred_domain TEXT, expires_at INTEGER`. This mirrors the SessionInfo extension landed by WO-0008. If the executor finds a contradiction with §11 in the future, raise an ADR — do not silently deviate.
TEST-DB PATH HYGIENE: `tempfile::TempDir::new()?.path().join("knowledge.db")` — never write to `/tmp` directly. Drop the TempDir at end of test for clean teardown.

CEQP TEST SHAPE: `let tools = ucil_tools(); let mut bad: Vec<String> = Vec::new(); for tool in &tools { let props = tool.input_schema.get("properties").and_then(Value::as_object).expect("properties"); for key in ["reason", "current_task", "files_in_context", "token_budget"] { if !props.contains_key(key) { bad.push(format!("{}.{}", tool.name, key)); } } } assert!(bad.is_empty(), "missing CEQP keys: {:?}", bad);` — collect-all-then-assert so the failure message lists every offender at once. Then add type-check assertions for each property (string/string/array/integer per §8.2).

IDEMPOTENCY CHECK in test_schema_creation: open the kg, drop it, re-open the same path, query `sqlite_master` for table count, assert it equals the count after first open (no duplicates, no errors). This catches `CREATE TABLE` without `IF NOT EXISTS`.

COMMIT CADENCE (target ~6-7 commits, each ≤150 lines of diff per DEC-0005): (1) `feat(core): add knowledge_graph module skeleton with KnowledgeGraph + Error`, (2) `feat(core): add §12.1 init DDL constant and BEGIN IMMEDIATE helper`, (3) `feat(core): apply WAL + busy_timeout + foreign_keys pragmas in KnowledgeGraph::open`, (4) `test(core): add module-level test_schema_creation covering pragmas + tables + idempotency`, (5) `chore(core): re-export KnowledgeGraph + KnowledgeGraphError from lib.rs`, (6) `test(daemon): add module-level test_ceqp_params_on_all_tools`, (7) `chore: clippy + fmt clean-up across both crates`. DEC-0005 allows a single oversized module-introduction commit when splitting would break compilation; if the const INIT_SQL pushes commit (2) past 200 lines, ship as one commit and reference DEC-0005 in the body.
NO `unwrap()` / `expect()` outside `#[cfg(test)]` (.claude/rules/rust-style.md). Use `?` and the typed `KnowledgeGraphError`. Tests may use `.expect("...")`.

decision:DEC-0005-WO-0006-module-coherence-commits.md — extends DEC-0001 to ucil-daemon and ucil-core single-file modules with coherent type+impl+tests
decision:DEC-0007-remove-cargo-mutants-per-wo-gate.md — cargo-mutants removed from per-WO gating; reality-check.sh remains the mutation oracle
escalation:20260415-1856-wo-WO-0006-attempts-exhausted.md — root cause 2 (frozen exact-match selector rejected inside `mod tests {}` wrapper); same lesson applies to BOTH tests in this WO
work-order:ucil-build/work-orders/0007-fix-storage-test-selector.json — precedent micro-WO that flattened `mod tests` for selector match
work-order:ucil-build/work-orders/0009-salsa-and-plugin-manager.json — precedent for adding new module to ucil-core with pub re-exports + module-prefix tests

lessons_applied: WO-0006/WO-0007/WO-0008/WO-0010 all taught: exact-match nextest selectors (`module::fn_name`) do NOT match tests nested inside `mod tests { }` blocks (which produce `module::tests::fn_name`). BOTH features in this WO use exact-match selectors — the plan calls out module-level placement explicitly in scope_in, in implementation_notes, AND adds two `grep -nE 'fn …'` acceptance criteria that the verifier (and the executor's local pre-check) can run.

DEC-0001/DEC-0005 precedent: the new `knowledge_graph.rs` is a single coherent module (types + impl + module-level test + optional `mod tests` for supporting tests). Splitting the const INIT_SQL across multiple commits would break compilation between them; one oversized commit is permitted under DEC-0005 and the executor should annotate the commit body with `Per: DEC-0005`.

DEC-0007: no cargo-mutants run is part of this WO's gate. Reality-check.sh stash-mutation is still in force; the executor should make sure stashing knowledge_graph.rs causes test_schema_creation to fail (per-file rollback may false-positive for single-file modules per escalation 20260415-1630 — manual verification by the verifier is the documented workaround).

WO-0008 / DEC-0007 lesson: while WO-0008 features (P1-W3-F01, P1-W4-F07) and WO-0006 features (P1-W2-F02, P1-W2-F03, P1-W2-F06) are still passes=false, both are awaiting verifier re-run on existing branches (escalations resolved). This WO does NOT pick up those features — the executor is forbidden from touching `crates/ucil-daemon/src/{storage,lifecycle,session_manager}.rs` or `crates/ucil-treesitter/**` to avoid merge conflicts. Once those branches merge, the next planner cycle will pick up the still-blocked downstream features (P1-W2-F04, P1-W3-F02, P1-W3-F08, P1-W3-F09, P1-W4-F02 …).

Avoid main.rs wiring: every Phase-1 WO so far has explicitly avoided editing `crates/ucil-daemon/src/main.rs`. The integration WO that wires Lifecycle + PluginManager + McpServer + KnowledgeGraph + watcher + indexing into a single startup sequence is reserved for P1-W3-F08 (progressive startup) — a separate WO once the prerequisite branches are merged.
planner_notes: P1-W4-F01 deps (P0-W1-F07 ✓) and P1-W4-F06 deps (P1-W3-F07 ✓) are both green on main. The two features share crate-foundation status: P1-W4-F01 lays the persistent-storage substrate that downstream W4 features (F02 CRUD, F03 symbol resolution, F04 tree-sitter→KG, F05 find_definition tool, F08 hot staging, F09 dedup, F10 _meta) all depend on; P1-W4-F06 closes the frozen-selector test gap that WO-0010 left open (the schema is wired but the named regression test was missing). WO-0006 / WO-0008 features remain on their feature branches awaiting verifier re-run after DEC-0007 + WO-0007 unblocks. This WO does NOT span >1 week of master-plan scope (both features are Week 4 per master-plan §18 lines 1747-1754) and does NOT touch any in-flight branch's files (forbidden_paths enumerate every such file). No ADR required: the SQLite schema is fully specified in master-plan §12.1; the only inferred element is the `sessions` table column set, which is derived directly from §11.2 + WO-0008's SessionInfo extension and called out in implementation_notes for transparency.
